component.name: Local AI Hub
component.summary: Control panel for your local AI stack

description.p: Local AI Hub is a free, open-source control panel for the AI services running on your own machine — Ollama, Open WebUI, and ComfyUI. It puts everything in one window so you don't need a terminal for day-to-day use.

ul.li: See what's running at a glance, with live status for each service.
Start and stop each tool with a single toggle.
Manage your models — update, install, and remove them.
Plain-language alerts when a service crashes, with a one-click log view.

description.p: Everything stays local. There's no account, no tracking, and no telemetry. The only network use is the model update checks and downloads you explicitly click.

It was built and verified on Bazzite with AMD Strix Halo hardware. Other setups are untested for now — the app is honest about what it can and can't detect on your machine, so services that aren't installed simply show as "Not installed" rather than erroring.

screenshot.caption: The hub in dark mode, showing running services and installed models

description.p: Relicensed under the GNU Affero General Public License v3.0, and added an in-app license line to the About panel.
First public release of Local AI Hub.
